name: Polenta (Pickarski)
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Olive oil, or corn oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in a large saucepan.
2. Saute the carrots, onions, celery, and garlic with salt, pepper, and basil for about 8 minutes.
3. Add the stewed tomatoes (sliced) to the vegetables along with the water. Bring to a simmer.
4. Add the corn grits or cornmeal and kidney beans; mix well.
5. Cook for about 20 minutes, stirring occasionally until the mixture reaches a soft, gritty texture.
6. Lightly oil a 2-quart mold and press the polenta into it, working out any air pockets.
7. Smooth the surface and allow to cool to room temperature.
8. Refrigerate the mold until cold, then unmold the polenta onto a tray.
9. Garnish and serve in slices.
Note: If using cornmeal instead of corn grits, soak the cornmeal separately in 2 cups of cold water; add the remaining 2 cups of water to the vegetables before combining them with the cornmeal mixture.
Variation: Millet Polenta - Use 2 cups millet and 5 cups water instead of corn grits and the 4 cups water.
